home.subtitle: I specialize in building apps using <bold>Next.js</bold>, <bold>React</bold>, and <bold>React Native</bold> to create seamless user experiences.
home.aboutP1: I'm a self-taught frontend developer based in Spain, passionate about building intuitive and performant web applications. I specialize in the React ecosystem, working daily with Next.js, TypeScript, and modern CSS tools like Tailwind.
home.aboutP2: With experience across fintech, real estate, and tech companies, I bring a versatile approach to frontend development. I care deeply about clean code, component architecture, and delivering great user experiences.
home.aboutP3: When I'm not coding, you can find me exploring new technologies, contributing to open source, or enjoying a good cup of coffee.
home.contactSubtitle: I'm always open to new opportunities and collaborations. Feel free to reach out!

notFound.title: Page not found
notFound.description: The page you're looking for doesn't exist or has been moved.

batatabit.shortDescription: Landing page for a cryptocurrency platform with real-time market info, designed with a mobile-first approach and CSS animations.
batatabit.description: Batatabit is a landing page for a cryptocurrency service that shows market trends, subscription plans, and a real-time API. The project was built with a mobile-first approach, ensuring a fluid experience across all devices.
batatabit.purpose: Practice mobile-first methodology and create a professional landing page with multiple sections, pricing tables, and an attractive design that prioritizes the mobile experience.
batatabit.stackExplanation: Semantic HTML for structure, CSS with media queries for mobile-first responsive design, and vanilla JavaScript for user interactions and scroll animations.
batatabit.challenges: The biggest challenge was making pricing tables and data sections look great on both mobile and desktop while maintaining readability and visual hierarchy without CSS frameworks.
batatabit.lessonsLearned: I learned the importance of designing for mobile first and scaling up. I also improved my understanding of media queries and how to structure CSS in a maintainable way.

crowdfund.shortDescription: Interactive crowdfunding page with pledge system, selection modal, and dynamic progress bar. Built with HTML, CSS, and vanilla JavaScript.
crowdfund.description: Crowdfund is an interactive crowdfunding landing page simulating a real product with a pledge system, reward selection modal, dynamic progress bar, and inventory state management. The project includes complex UI interactions.
crowdfund.purpose: Develop an interactive application with complex state management using only vanilla JavaScript, including modals, form validation, and dynamic UI updates.
crowdfund.stackExplanation: HTML for semantic structure, CSS custom properties for consistent theming, and vanilla JavaScript for all business logic: pledge management, inventory control, and DOM updates.
crowdfund.challenges: Implementing state management without frameworks was the main challenge. The modal needed to sync data between the selected pledge, available inventory, and the progress bar in real time.
crowdfund.lessonsLearned: I better understood state management patterns in vanilla JavaScript and the importance of separating business logic from DOM manipulation. This prepared me to better understand frameworks like React.

loopstudio.shortDescription: Landing page for a virtual reality studio with responsive navigation, project grid, and animations. Modern design with CSS Grid and Flexbox.
loopstudio.description: Loopstudio is a landing page for an immersive virtual reality experience studio. It includes responsive navigation with a hamburger menu, hero section with background image, project grid with hover effects, and a complete footer. Design based on a Frontend Mentor challenge.
loopstudio.purpose: Master CSS Grid for complex gallery layouts and practice implementing responsive navigation with an animated mobile menu.
loopstudio.stackExplanation: Semantic HTML5, CSS Grid for the project gallery, Flexbox for alignments, and JavaScript for the hamburger menu and scroll animations.
loopstudio.challenges: Getting the project grid to adapt correctly between mobile (1 column) and desktop (4 columns) while maintaining image proportions and text overlays was the biggest challenge.
loopstudio.lessonsLearned: CSS Grid is extremely powerful for gallery layouts. I learned to combine Grid with object-fit and position to create elegant cards with text overlays.

sunnyside.shortDescription: Landing page for a creative agency with service sections, client testimonials, and an image gallery. Colorful and modern design.
sunnyside.description: Sunnyside Agency is a landing page for a creative agency showcasing its services through impactful visual sections, client testimonials, and an image gallery. The project stands out for its use of color and visual composition. Frontend Mentor challenge.
sunnyside.purpose: Create a visually rich landing page with multiple content sections, practicing the layout of complex designs with images and overlaid text.
sunnyside.stackExplanation: Semantic HTML for accessibility, CSS with advanced positioning techniques for overlaying text on images, and JavaScript for mobile navigation and interactions.
sunnyside.challenges: The design required overlaying text on background images in a readable way across all screen sizes. Alternating image-text sections needed to change layout between mobile and desktop.
sunnyside.lessonsLearned: I improved my ability to lay out complex designs with multiple breakpoints. I also learned techniques for maintaining text readability over variable background images.

portfolio.shortDescription: This very portfolio! Built with React 18, TypeScript, Vite, Tailwind CSS, and Framer Motion. Features dark mode, i18n, and page transitions.
portfolio.description: My personal portfolio website built with modern frontend technologies. Features bilingual support (EN/ES), dark mode, page transitions with Framer Motion, responsive design, SEO optimization, and a clean component architecture.
portfolio.purpose: Create a professional portfolio to showcase my work and skills, while demonstrating proficiency with modern frontend technologies like React, TypeScript, and Tailwind CSS.
portfolio.stackExplanation: React 18 with TypeScript for type safety, Vite for fast builds, Tailwind CSS for utility-first styling, Framer Motion for smooth animations, react-i18next for internationalization, and react-helmet-async for SEO.
portfolio.challenges: Implementing bilingual support across the entire site while maintaining clean code organization was the main challenge. Also, designing a portfolio that effectively showcases frontend skills without relying on complex backend services.
portfolio.lessonsLearned: I learned how to architect a React application for scalability, implement i18n from the ground up, and create a cohesive design system with Tailwind CSS.
